回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	112學年度第1學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 英文寫作(三) (English Writing III)	開課班級 : 應英二	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 陳宛利	必選修 : 必修	 	 


1.教學目標
a) Write efficient descriptions that “show” the reader what is happening, rather than “tell” the reader (“show” rather than “tell”) b) Learn the power of poetic and descriptive devices in allowing the reader to connect to the writing, including action verbs, rhyme, imagery, metaphors, and sensory details c) Use poetry to foster reading fluency and oral presentation skills (poetry reading) d) Write collaboratively and self-expressively e) Learn the structures and forms of English poetry, and adapt to these with creativity f) Experience various writing processes: brainstorming, prewriting, drafting, peer-reviewing, and revising  


2.教學綱要
Designed for advanced ESL writers, this writing course introduces a unique literary genre, poetry, to facilitate the goal that writing serves as both a social means of self-representation and a personal tool to inquire into real-life issues. Throughout this semester students will read a variety of poems, composed by writers of different language backgrounds, analyze poetic devices in these creative works, and adapt to these with creativity in their own poems. At the end of this class students will compose a collection of poems that reflect their unique perspectives. The ultimate goal of this class is to allow students to utilize writing as a dialogic thinking space and to have a sense of ownership in the learning experience.

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Assessment: Attendance, in-class performance, poetry recitation & presentation (40%); Midterm presentation (30%); Final portfolio (30%) 
Course Requirements: Students are expected to attend all the class sessions and attend each class with the course readings. Late arrival, coming to class empty-handed, or being a phubber in class will lead to a deduction of the final grade. Be on time and come prepared to learn and contribute. Students will be selected at random to answer questions and be graded based on how well they demonstrate understandings of the reading. Understand and be responsible for your grade. 
To facilitate learning outside the classroom, course materials and assignments will be uploaded onto E-campus, an E-learning environment for all NQU courses. All students must register for E-campus in order to gain full access to the updated course information and submit homework online. For course-related issues or questions, contact me at tmpmlily@gmail.com * 詳盡之授課規劃將於第一週上課公告及說明，並依照實際上課狀況與(遠距)教學形式作適度調整，請同學至Ecampus平台，課程內輸入Email信箱，以接收即時課程資訊。
